metadata.title: CAST Docs
metadata.description: Render engineering documents from JSON to self-contained HTML. Component Assembly Styled Templates.

items.label: Format
items.body: Author engineering documents as structured JSON; render to one self-contained HTML file.

items.label: Output
items.body: Inline CSS, no CDN, no external scripts. Opens in any browser, attaches to email, archives, and publishes to GitHub Pages.

items.label: Use as
items.body: A Claude Code skill, or a standalone Python renderer with zero third-party dependencies.

blocks.text: CAST Docs is a controlled replacement for Markdown-based engineering documents. The JSON is the source. The HTML is the artifact. The renderer enforces one visual system, one class vocabulary, and one validation contract across every output.

Each scenario picks a section order and a set of components that fit a common writing situation. Open any preview to see a rendered example; the matching JSON fixture lives next to it in the repository.

items.label: Problem investigation
items.text: Tracking down a bug, regression, or anomaly.

items.label: Option decision
items.text: Comparing options and recording why one was chosen.

items.label: Cross-team alignment
items.text: Aligning multiple teams on scope and ownership.

items.label: Document digest
items.text: Summarizing a long document or set of documents.

items.label: Principle showcase
items.text: Curating principles or guidelines for reuse.

items.title: HTML, not Markdown
items.body: Markdown cannot carry callouts, decision matrices, diff blocks, diagram viewers, and controlled tables without per-renderer extensions. Owning the HTML produces consistent output across every viewer.

items.title: JSON intermediate
items.body: Prose to HTML by a model is non-deterministic. Prose to JSON to HTML separates content from rendering, keeps the JSON validatable and diffable, and lets the renderer produce byte-stable output.

items.title: Self-contained output
items.body: Inline CSS, no external assets. The HTML survives being opened in any browser, attached to email, copied to S3, or served from GitHub Pages.

items.title: Config-driven renderer
items.body: Adding a new component, theme, layout, or interaction is config plus an asset file, not a Python branch. Visual and structural rules live in config/. The Python in scripts/ only assembles.

blocks.text: Requires Python 3.9 or newer. Standard library only. Clone the repository and render one of the bundled examples:

blocks.code:
git clone https://github.com/CAST-docs/cast-docs.git
cd cast-docs
python3 scripts/render_html.py \
  --input examples/problem-investigation.json \
  --output out.html \
  --validate

blocks.title: Use as a Claude Code skill
blocks.body: Clone into ~/.claude/skills/cast-docs and the skill activates when you ask Claude for an engineering spec, decision record, or self-contained HTML document. References in references/ are loaded on demand.

items.label: JSON schema
items.text: The contract every input document must satisfy.

items.label: Design references
items.text: Design laws, generation contract, module architecture, interactive features.

items.title: Done (P0)
items.body: Single-doc rendering with the full block library. cast-default theme compiled from theme tokens. Diagram viewer interaction with zoom, pan, SVG and PNG download. JSON schema and HTML profile validation.

items.title: Planned
items.body: Document-set layout with sidebar, topbar, and pagination. Index builder for GitHub-Pages-friendly multi-page sites. Typography and spacing tokens compiled into the base CSS. Renderer-generated example HTML to replace any remaining hand-authored samples.
